behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification judgment on explicit content, not just routing. A stubbed classifier would hide misclassification failures; no real OS/network behavior is required beyond normal service wiring, and this is a single model-driven decision (not a multi-agent chain). Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior text describes one moderation judgment, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the moderation path with the real vision model on a curated explicit-image set plus safe controls; assert explicit samples are flagged and placed in review hold state, and none are published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real text-model robustness to obfuscation patterns, which is model-output quality. Deterministic mocks always miss this failure class. Rejected Workflow: this is one model classification task, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks) through the real text moderation model and assert they are flagged/held; include benign near-neighbors to detect overblocking.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This targets cross-module handoff and decision orchestration after both checks return pass. The failure is in local service/repo/state transitions (publish vs hold/drop), which in-process wiring can expose. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess conditions. Rejected Agentic: text says "passes both content checks," so this test validates post-check orchestration, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator, storage, and publish queue with deterministic pass results from image/text check modules; assert resulting state is published exactly once and never held/dropped.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO under throughput pressure, which depends on real runtime/process/network behavior and queueing effects. In-process mocks can hide true timing bottlenecks. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: requirement is timing completion, not correctness of model semantic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through the deployed moderation service under representative peak load with real HTTP/service boundaries; measure p95/p99 completion time from submit to final decision and verify <=5s target.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is external dependency unavailability (timeouts/connection errors/status failures) and fallback behavior under real service conditions. Pure mocks often miss transport/error-mode edge cases. Rejected Agentic: this is availability/error-handling infrastructure, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service unavailability (e.g., endpoint blackhole/timeout/5xx) in a system environment; submit image posts and assert they enter retry queue with no auto-approval path taken.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This requires multiple real agentic decisions in sequence over repeated submissions, then checking consistency across those steps. A single-call agentic test is insufficient for this temporal/repetition failure mode. Rejected lower Agentic: behavior text explicitly requires repeated submissions "multiple times within a short window," i.e., multi-step sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly (e.g., 10-20 times) within the defined short window through the real moderation pipeline and verify decision consistency criteria across attempts (same hold/publish outcome or bounded variance policy).
